skill.name: github-pr-merge
skill.description: Guides GitHub pull request merges with pre-merge checks, user confirmation, merge message formatting, and branch cleanup.
skill.summary: Validate and merge GitHub pull requests with a repeatable checklist.
skill.author: Bae-ChangHyun

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are true positives because the workflow instructs agents to run GitHub CLI, make, and git commands that can merge PRs and delete branches. The weak cryptographic algorithm and reconnaissance alerts are false positives in context; no cryptography, malicious system probing, or unrelated network scanning was found. A high-risk prompt-control phrase in the skill metadata requires revision before publication.

high_findings.title: Prompt Control Language in Skill Metadata
high_findings.description: The description says the skill must be used and that it overrides default PR merge behavior. This can pressure an agent to prioritize the skill over normal policy, routing, or user-intent checks.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are directly present in the skill metadata. The text does not ask to bypass security review, so this is high risk but not confirmed malware.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Changing External Commands
medium_findings.description: The workflow instructs agents to merge pull requests, delete remote branches, pull develop, and delete local branches. These actions are legitimate for the skill purpose but can cause irreversible repository changes if used on the wrong PR or branch.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact gh and git commands are shown in the workflow. Their purpose is legitimate, but the side effects are material and require explicit user authorization.

medium_findings.title: Shell Variable Expansion in Command Examples
medium_findings.description: The command examples build GitHub API paths and merge commands using shell variables such as PR and REPO. The values appear to come from GitHub CLI output, but the examples do not consistently quote variables.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation is visible, but the values are normally constrained by GitHub CLI and GitHub repository naming rules. The risk is lower than arbitrary user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alert Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at the metadata description line. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or encryption operation is present at that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain skill metadata describing a PR merge workflow. I did not find evidence of weak cryptographic behavior in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Alerts Match GitHub PR Inspection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system and network reconnaissance. The referenced commands inspect the active repository and PR comments through GitHub CLI for merge readiness, not host or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands query GitHub pull request comments or stop the merge process. I did not find evidence of unrelated host enumeration or network scanning.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides executable shell examples for make, gh, and git. These are expected for a merge workflow but require a trusted repository and user confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit bash blocks with external commands. The commands align with the skill purpose, but the execution risk is real.

content.value_statement: Merging pull requests can miss failed checks, unresolved comments, or incomplete release notes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured checklist for safer GitHub PR merges.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the current GitHub pull request and repository with GitHub CLI commands.
Runs test, lint, and PR check commands before merge.
Checks whether original PR review comments have replies.
Prompts for user confirmation before executing the merge.
Formats a concise merge message with review, test, and task details.
Deletes merged branches and updates the develop branch after merge.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI authentication and repository permissions.
Assumes the project supports make test and make lint commands.
Does not resolve review comments or reply to reviewers.
Uses merge commits and does not support squash or rebase workflows.

use_cases.title: Release Engineer PR Finalization
use_cases.description: Validate checks, confirm review status, and merge a ready pull request with a consistent message.

use_cases.title: Team Lead Review Gate
use_cases.description: Apply a repeatable checklist before merging team changes into a shared branch.

use_cases.title: Solo Maintainer Cleanup
use_cases.description: Merge approved work and clean up local and remote feature branches after confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Current PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the current GitHub pull request and merge it only after I confirm.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a guarded merge workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Check Merge Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check tests, lint, CI status, and review comments for the current PR. Tell me whether it is ready to merge.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pre-merge readiness review without immediate merge.

prompt_templates.title: Merge With Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a concise merge message for this PR, show the validation results, and ask before merging.
prompt_templates.scenario: Controlled merge with a human-readable merge body.

prompt_templates.title: Finalize Reviewed PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PR merge workflow for this reviewed branch. Verify comments, run validation, merge with a merge commit, and clean up branches after approval.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with validation and post-merge cleanup.

output_examples.input: Merge this PR after checking it.
output_examples.output: The skill reports passing tests, lint, and CI checks, then asks for confirmation before merging the PR.

output_examples.input: Is this PR ready to merge?
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes PR title, commit count, changed files, comment status, and any blockers.
The skill merges the approved PR with a concise message, deletes the remote branch, updates develop, and removes the local branch.

best_practices: Run the workflow only in a trusted repository with the correct active branch.
Review the PR number, target branch, and merge message before approving.
Keep GitHub CLI authentication scoped to the repositories you manage.
anti_patterns: Do not merge when tests, lint, CI, or review comments are unresolved.
Do not use this workflow for repositories that require squash or rebase merges.
Do not run cleanup commands before confirming the merged branch names.

faq.question: Does this skill merge pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to ask for user confirmation before the merge command.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It requires GitHub CLI, git, and project commands such as make test and make lint.

faq.question: Can it handle unresolved review comments?
faq.answer: No. It stops the merge and directs the user to resolve comments first.

faq.question: Does it support squash merges?
faq.answer: No. The workflow specifies merge commits with the GitHub CLI merge option.

faq.question: Can it delete branches?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes remote branch deletion after merge and local branch cleanup.

faq.question: Is it safe for all repositories?
faq.answer: No. Use it only where merge commits and branch deletion match the repository policy.
